fields.name: clouderrorreporting_projects_events_list

args.name: groupId
args.description: Required. The group for which events shall be returned. The `group_id` is a unique identifier for a particular error group. The identifier is derived from key parts of the error-log content and is treated as Service Data. For information about how Service Data is handled, see [Google Cloud Privacy Notice](https://cloud.google.com/terms/cloud-privacy-notice).

args.name: pageSize

args.name: serviceFilter_service
args.description: Optional. The exact value to match against [`ServiceContext.service`](/error-reporting/reference/rest/v1beta1/ServiceContext#FIELDS.service).

args.name: serviceFilter_version
args.description: Optional. The exact value to match against [`ServiceContext.version`](/error-reporting/reference/rest/v1beta1/ServiceContext#FIELDS.version).

args.name: serviceFilter_resourceType
args.description: Optional. The exact value to match against [`ServiceContext.resource_type`](/error-reporting/reference/rest/v1beta1/ServiceContext#FIELDS.resource_type).

args.name: timeRange_period
args.description: Restricts the query to the specified time range.

args.name: timedCountDuration
args.description: Optional. The preferred duration for a single returned TimedCount. If not set, no timed counts are returned.

args.name: alignment
args.description: Optional. The alignment of the timed counts to be returned. Default is `ALIGNMENT_EQUAL_AT_END`.

args.name: alignmentTime
args.description: Optional. Time where the timed counts shall be aligned if rounded alignment is chosen. Default is 00:00 UTC.

args.name: order
args.description: Optional. The sort order in which the results are returned. Default is `COUNT_DESC`.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to return per response. Default is 20.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. A next_page_token provided by a previous response. To view additional results, pass this token along with the identical query parameters as the first request.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: ListEventsResponse
types.description: Contains a set of requested error events.

fields.name: errorEvents
fields.description: The error events which match the given request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: If non-empty, more results are available. Pass this token, along with the same query parameters as the first request, to view the next page of results.

fields.name: timeRangeBegin
fields.description: The timestamp specifies the start time to which the request was restricted.

types.name: ErrorEvent
types.description: An error event which is returned by the Error Reporting system.

fields.name: eventTime
fields.description: Time when the event occurred as provided in the error report. If the report did not contain a timestamp, the time the error was received by the Error Reporting system is used.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The stack trace that was reported or logged by the service.

types.name: ServiceContext
types.description: Describes a running service that sends errors. Its version changes over time and multiple versions can run in parallel.

fields.name: service
fields.description: An identifier of the service, such as the name of the executable, job, or Google App Engine service name. This field is expected to have a low number of values that are relatively stable over time, as opposed to `version`, which can be changed whenever new code is deployed. Contains the service name for error reports extracted from Google App Engine logs or `default` if the App Engine default service is used.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Represents the source code version that the developer provided, which could represent a version label or a Git SHA-1 hash, for example. For App Engine standard environment, the version is set to the version of the app.

fields.name: resourceType
fields.description: Type of the MonitoredResource. List of possible values: https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/resources Value is set automatically for incoming errors and must not be set when reporting errors.

types.name: ErrorContext
types.description: A description of the context in which an error occurred. This data should be provided by the application when reporting an error, unless the error report has been generated automatically from Google App Engine logs.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user who caused or was affected by the crash. This can be a user ID, an email address, or an arbitrary token that uniquely identifies the user. When sending an error report, leave this field empty if the user was not logged in. In this case the Error Reporting system will use other data, such as remote IP address, to distinguish affected users. See `affected_users_count` in `ErrorGroupStats`.

fields.name: sourceReferences
fields.description: Source code that was used to build the executable which has caused the given error message.

types.name: HttpRequestContext
types.description: HTTP request data that is related to a reported error. This data should be provided by the application when reporting an error, unless the error report has been generated automatically from Google App Engine logs.

fields.name: method
fields.description: The type of HTTP request, such as `GET`, `POST`, etc.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the request.

fields.name: userAgent
fields.description: The user agent information that is provided with the request.

fields.name: referrer
fields.description: The referrer information that is provided with the request.

fields.name: responseStatusCode
fields.description: The HTTP response status code for the request.

fields.name: remoteIp
fields.description: The IP address from which the request originated. This can be IPv4, IPv6, or a token which is derived from the IP address, depending on the data that has been provided in the error report.

types.name: SourceLocation
types.description: Indicates a location in the source code of the service for which errors are reported. `functionName` must be provided by the application when reporting an error, unless the error report contains a `message` with a supported exception stack trace. All fields are optional for the later case.

fields.name: filePath
fields.description: The source code filename, which can include a truncated relative path, or a full path from a production machine.

fields.name: lineNumber
fields.description: 1-based. 0 indicates that the line number is unknown.

fields.name: functionName
fields.description: Human-readable name of a function or method. The value can include optional context like the class or package name. For example, `my.package.MyClass.method` in case of Java.

types.name: SourceReference
types.description: A reference to a particular snapshot of the source tree used to build and deploy an application.

fields.name: repository
fields.description: Optional. A URI string identifying the repository. Example: "https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/kubernetes.git"

fields.name: revisionId
fields.description: The canonical and persistent identifier of the deployed revision. Example (git): "0035781c50ec7aa23385dc841529ce8a4b70db1b"

types.name: ErrorGroup
types.description: Description of a group of similar error events.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The group resource name. Written as `projects/{projectID}/groups/{group_id}` or `projects/{projectID}/locations/{location}/groups/{group_id}` Examples: `projects/my-project-123/groups/my-group`, `projects/my-project-123/locations/us-central1/groups/my-group` In the group resource name, the `group_id` is a unique identifier for a particular error group. The identifier is derived from key parts of the error-log content and is treated as Service Data. For information about how Service Data is handled, see [Google Cloud Privacy Notice](https://cloud.google.com/terms/cloud-privacy-notice). For a list of supported locations, see [Supported Regions](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/region-support). `global` is the default when unspecified.

fields.name: groupId
fields.description: An opaque identifier of the group. This field is assigned by the Error Reporting system and always populated. In the group resource name, the `group_id` is a unique identifier for a particular error group. The identifier is derived from key parts of the error-log content and is treated as Service Data. For information about how Service Data is handled, see [Google Cloud Privacy Notice](https://cloud.google.com/terms/cloud-privacy-notice).

fields.name: trackingIssues
fields.description: Associated tracking issues.

types.name: TrackingIssue
types.description: Information related to tracking the progress on resolving the error.

fields.name: url
fields.description: A URL pointing to a related entry in an issue tracking system. Example: `https://github.com/user/project/issues/4`

types.name: mutation_clouderrorreporting_projects_groups_update_resolutionStatus
types.description: Error group's resolution status. An unspecified resolution status will be interpreted as OPEN

types.name: ListGroupStatsResponse
types.description: Contains a set of requested error group stats.

fields.name: errorGroupStats
fields.description: The error group stats which match the given request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: If non-empty, more results are available. Pass this token, along with the same query parameters as the first request, to view the next page of results.

fields.name: timeRangeBegin
fields.description: The timestamp specifies the start time to which the request was restricted. The start time is set based on the requested time range. It may be adjusted to a later time if a project has exceeded the storage quota and older data has been deleted.

types.name: ErrorGroupStats
types.description: Data extracted for a specific group based on certain filter criteria, such as a given time period and/or service filter.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Approximate total number of events in the given group that match the filter criteria.

fields.name: affectedUsersCount
fields.description: Approximate number of affected users in the given group that match the filter criteria. Users are distinguished by data in the ErrorContext of the individual error events, such as their login name or their remote IP address in case of HTTP requests. The number of affected users can be zero even if the number of errors is non-zero if no data was provided from which the affected user could be deduced. Users are counted based on data in the request context that was provided in the error report. If more users are implicitly affected, such as due to a crash of the whole service, this is not reflected here.

fields.name: timedCounts
fields.description: Approximate number of occurrences over time. Timed counts returned by ListGroups are guaranteed to be: - Inside the requested time interval - Non-overlapping, and - Ordered by ascending time.

fields.name: firstSeenTime
fields.description: Approximate first occurrence that was ever seen for this group and which matches the given filter criteria, ignoring the time_range that was specified in the request.

fields.name: lastSeenTime
fields.description: Approximate last occurrence that was ever seen for this group and which matches the given filter criteria, ignoring the time_range that was specified in the request.

fields.name: affectedServices
fields.description: Service contexts with a non-zero error count for the given filter criteria. This list can be truncated if multiple services are affected. Refer to `num_affected_services` for the total count.

fields.name: numAffectedServices
fields.description: The total number of services with a non-zero error count for the given filter criteria.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: TimedCount
types.description: The number of errors in a given time period. All numbers are approximate since the error events are sampled before counting them.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Approximate number of occurrences in the given time period.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Start of the time period to which `count` refers (included).

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: End of the time period to which `count` refers (excluded).

fields.name: clouderrorreporting_projects_deleteEvents
fields.description: Deletes all error events of a given project.

args.name: projectsId

fields.name: clouderrorreporting_projects_groups_update
fields.description: Replace the data for the specified group. Fails if the group does not exist.

args.name: projectsId
args.description: Path parameter projectsId

args.name: groupsId
args.description: Path parameter groupsId

fields.name: clouderrorreporting_projects_events_report
fields.description: Report an individual error event and record the event to a log. This endpoint accepts **either** an OAuth token, **or** an [API key](https://support.google.com/cloud/answer/6158862) for authentication. To use an API key, append it to the URL as the value of a `key` parameter. For example: `POST https://clouderrorreporting.googleapis.com/v1beta1/{projectName}/events:report?key=123ABC456` **Note:** [Error Reporting] (https://cloud.google.com/error-reporting) is a service built on Cloud Logging and can analyze log entries when all of the following are true: * Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) are disabled on the log bucket. * The log bucket satisfies one of the following: * The log bucket is stored in the same project where the logs originated. * The logs were routed to a project, and then that project stored those logs in a log bucket that it owns.

args.name: projectsId
args.description: Path parameter projectsId

fields.name: clouderrorreporting_projects_locations_deleteEvents
fields.description: Deletes all error events of a given project.

args.name: projectsId
args.description: Path parameter projectsId

args.name: locationsId
args.description: Path parameter locationsId

fields.description: Replace the data for the specified group. Fails if the group does not exist.

args.description: Path parameter projectsId

args.name: locationsId
args.description: Path parameter locationsId

args.name: groupsId
args.description: Path parameter groupsId

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: ErrorGroup_Input
types.description: Description of a group of similar error events.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The group resource name. Written as `projects/{projectID}/groups/{group_id}` or `projects/{projectID}/locations/{location}/groups/{group_id}` Examples: `projects/my-project-123/groups/my-group`, `projects/my-project-123/locations/us-central1/groups/my-group` In the group resource name, the `group_id` is a unique identifier for a particular error group. The identifier is derived from key parts of the error-log content and is treated as Service Data. For information about how Service Data is handled, see [Google Cloud Privacy Notice](https://cloud.google.com/terms/cloud-privacy-notice). For a list of supported locations, see [Supported Regions](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/region-support). `global` is the default when unspecified.

inputFields.name: groupId
inputFields.description: An opaque identifier of the group. This field is assigned by the Error Reporting system and always populated. In the group resource name, the `group_id` is a unique identifier for a particular error group. The identifier is derived from key parts of the error-log content and is treated as Service Data. For information about how Service Data is handled, see [Google Cloud Privacy Notice](https://cloud.google.com/terms/cloud-privacy-notice).

inputFields.name: trackingIssues
inputFields.description: Associated tracking issues.

types.name: TrackingIssue_Input
types.description: Information related to tracking the progress on resolving the error.

inputFields.name: url
inputFields.description: A URL pointing to a related entry in an issue tracking system. Example: `https://github.com/user/project/issues/4`

types.name: ReportedErrorEvent_Input
types.description: An error event which is reported to the Error Reporting system.

inputFields.name: eventTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Time when the event occurred. If not provided, the time when the event was received by the Error Reporting system is used. If provided, the time must not exceed the [logs retention period](https://cloud.google.com/logging/quotas#logs_retention_periods) in the past, or be more than 24 hours in the future. If an invalid time is provided, then an error is returned.

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: Required. The error message. If no `context.reportLocation` is provided, the message must contain a header (typically consisting of the exception type name and an error message) and an exception stack trace in one of the supported programming languages and formats. Supported languages are Java, Python, JavaScript, Ruby, C#, PHP, and Go. Supported stack trace formats are: * **Java**: Must be the return value of [`Throwable.printStackTrace()`](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Throwable.html#printStackTrace%28%29). * **Python**: Must be the return value of [`traceback.format_exc()`](https://docs.python.org/2/library/traceback.html#traceback.format_exc). * **JavaScript**: Must be the value of [`error.stack`](https://github.com/v8/v8/wiki/Stack-Trace-API) as returned by V8. * **Ruby**: Must contain frames returned by [`Exception.backtrace`](https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.2.0/Exception.html#method-i-backtrace). * **C#**: Must be the return value of [`Exception.ToString()`](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/system.exception.tostring.aspx). * **PHP**: Must be prefixed with `"PHP (Notice|Parse error|Fatal error|Warning): "` and contain the result of [`(string)$exception`](https://php.net/manual/en/exception.tostring.php). * **Go**: Must be the return value of [`debug.Stack()`](https://pkg.go.dev/runtime/debug#Stack).

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
